title: Replace a lost or stolen Smart ID / ID book
summary: Report the loss and apply for a replacement identity document.
whoFor: Any South African citizen whose ID has been lost, stolen, or damaged.
documents: Sworn affidavit or SAPS case number confirming the loss/theft
Your original birth certificate (if you have it)
Proof of residential address (utility bill, lease, or affidavit)
Passport-style photos (only required at some offices — check first)
steps: Report the loss at a police station and get a case number, or complete a sworn affidavit.
Visit your nearest Home Affairs office and complete Form BI-9 (application for an identity document).
Have your fingerprints and photo taken at the office.
Pay the applicable fee (a first replacement may differ in cost from repeat replacements).
Track your application status using the reference slip you're given, either in person, by SMS, or online.
cost: A fee applies for replacement IDs; first-time IDs for citizens turning 16 are free. Fees change from time to time.
processingTime: Typically a few weeks, but can vary significantly by office and season.

title: Apply for UIF (Unemployment Insurance Fund) benefits
summary: Claim unemployment benefits after losing a job, or maternity/illness benefits while employed.
whoFor: Employees who have lost their job, are on maternity leave, or are unable to work due to illness, and whose employer contributed to UIF.
documents: 13-digit bar-coded ID or Smart ID card
UI-19 form completed by your last employer
Proof of registration as a work seeker (for unemployment claims)
Bank statement not older than 3 months
Salary schedule / last payslips
A completed UI-2.8 bank form for payment details
steps: Register on the uFiling online portal, or visit your nearest Labour Centre in person.
Get your employer to complete and submit a UI-19 form declaring your last date of employment.
Complete the relevant claim form for your situation (unemployment, maternity, illness, adoption, or dependent's benefits).
Submit supporting documents, including your bank details.
Follow up periodically (in person or via uFiling) — you generally need to reconfirm continued unemployment roughly every four weeks.
cost: Free to apply. UIF is funded by monthly contributions already deducted from your salary while employed.
processingTime: Payments typically begin a few weeks after a complete application, but delays are common if documents are missing.

title: Register a company or business (CIPC)
summary: Register a private company (Pty Ltd) or other business entity with the Companies and Intellectual Property Commission.
whoFor: Anyone starting a formal business in South Africa, from a sole proprietor formalising a company to a group of founders.
documents: South African ID or passport (for foreign directors) for all directors/incorporators
Proposed company name(s), in order of preference
A registered address for the company
Payment for the name reservation and registration fees
steps: Register for a CIPC customer code and a BizPortal or CIPC eServices account.
Reserve your company name (optional but recommended) or register with a name based on your registration number.
Complete the company registration (Form COR14.1) via BizPortal, which can also help you register for tax, UIF, and Compensation Fund at the same time.
Pay the registration fee.
Receive your company registration certificate (COR14.3) and registration number once approved.
cost: Company registration fees are low-cost; name reservation carries a small separate fee. BizPortal bundles some registrations for a single combined price.
processingTime: Often same-day to a few days when documents are in order.

title: Report a municipal service issue (water, electricity, roads, refuse)
summary: Log a fault or complaint about municipal infrastructure such as water leaks, power outages, potholes, or missed refuse collection.
whoFor: Any resident or ratepayer experiencing a service delivery problem in their municipality.
documents: Your municipal account number (if reporting a billing-related fault)
The exact address or GPS location of the problem
Photos of the issue, where possible
A reference number from any earlier report on the same issue
steps: Identify the correct municipality for your area and find its official website or app.
Use the municipality's call centre, official app, website form, or social media channel to log the fault.
Note the reference or case number you're given — you'll need it to follow up.
Escalate through your ward councillor's office if the issue is not resolved within a reasonable time.
For provincial or national roads, report separately to the relevant roads agency rather than the local municipality.
processingTime: Varies widely by municipality and issue type — from hours for urgent safety hazards to weeks for infrastructure repairs.

title: Register a birth or apply for a birth certificate
summary: Register a child's birth (unabridged or abridged) or request a copy of an existing birth certificate.
whoFor: Parents registering a new birth, or anyone needing a duplicate or unabridged certificate for themselves or a dependant.
documents: Both parents' ID documents (or the available parent's ID, plus supporting affidavits if one parent is absent)
Notice of Birth (BI-24) issued by the hospital or clinic where the child was born
Proof of marriage, if applicable
Proof of residential address
steps: Register the birth within 30 days at a Home Affairs office (free within this period; late registration involves an additional process).
Complete the birth registration form with both parents present, where possible.
Collect the unabridged birth certificate once processed, or apply later for a duplicate if the original is lost.
For late registrations (after 30 days), be prepared for additional verification, which can take considerably longer.
cost: Free within 30 days of birth. Late registration and duplicate certificates carry a fee.
processingTime: On-time registrations can be same-day for the notice, with the certificate following; late registrations can take months due to verification.

title: Apply for or renew a South African passport
summary: Apply for a new passport or renew an expiring one for travel purposes.
whoFor: South African citizens needing a passport for international travel.
documents: Green bar-coded ID book or Smart ID card
Your current passport, if renewing
Passport-sized photos (only if your office doesn't capture these digitally)
Proof of payment for the application fee
steps: Book an appointment where available, or visit a Home Affairs office that offers passport services.
Complete the passport application form (BI-73) in person.
Have your biometrics (photo and fingerprints) captured at the office.
Pay the applicable fee and keep your receipt with reference number.
Track the application status online or by SMS using your reference number, and collect your passport once ready.
cost: A standard fee applies; fees are higher for urgent/express applications where available.
processingTime: Standard applications typically take a few weeks; express services, where offered, are faster at a higher cost.

title: Apply for a SASSA social grant
summary: Apply for a social grant such as the Older Person's Grant, Disability Grant, Child Support Grant, or SRD grant.
whoFor: South African citizens, permanent residents, or refugees who meet the means test and eligibility criteria for a specific grant.
documents: 13-digit bar-coded ID or Smart ID card
Proof of income (or lack thereof) for the means test
Proof of residential address
Supporting documents specific to the grant (e.g. child's birth certificate for Child Support, medical assessment for Disability Grant)
Bank account details for payment
steps: Confirm which grant you qualify for and check the current means-test income thresholds.
Gather the required supporting documents for that specific grant type.
Apply in person at your nearest SASSA office, or online/via the SASSA app where the specific grant supports it (such as the SRD grant).
Complete a medical assessment where required, such as for the Disability Grant.
Wait for the outcome notification and, once approved, collect payment via your chosen payment method.
processingTime: Varies by grant type; some online applications (like SRD) give faster status feedback than in-person applications for other grants.

title: Renew a driver's licence card
summary: Renew an expiring or expired driver's licence card at a licensing centre.
whoFor: Licensed drivers whose driver's licence card is due to expire or has already expired.
documents: Current driver's licence card
ID document or Smart ID card
Recent eye-test results or willingness to complete one on-site
Passport photos, if required by your specific licensing centre
steps: Check your card's expiry date — renewal can usually be done up to a few months in advance.
Book an appointment at a licensing centre, if the centre requires bookings.
Complete the renewal application form and pass a basic eye test.
Have your photo and fingerprints captured.
Pay the renewal fee and collect a temporary authority to drive while the new card is produced.
cost: A standard renewal fee applies; expired-licence renewals may attract a penalty depending on how overdue they are.
processingTime: The temporary authority is usually issued the same day; the physical card can take several weeks to a few months to arrive.

title: Register for a tax number and file a tax return
summary: Get a personal income tax number and understand the basics of filing an annual return with SARS.
whoFor: Anyone earning above the tax threshold, starting formal employment, or needing a tax number for other official purposes.
documents: ID document or Smart ID card
Proof of residential address
Proof of banking details
IRP5/IT3(a) certificate(s) from your employer(s) for filing season
Any other income or deduction certificates (medical aid, retirement annuity, etc.)
steps: Register for a tax number via SARS eFiling, the SARS MobiApp, or in person at a SARS branch (many employees are auto-registered when they start working).
Activate your eFiling profile to submit returns and communicate with SARS online.
During filing season, gather your IRP5 and other certificates.
Complete and submit your return via eFiling or the MobiApp, checking the auto-assessment SARS may have already prepared for you.
Pay any amount owed, or track a refund, through your eFiling profile.
cost: Free to register and to file a standard return.
processingTime: Auto-assessments are often issued within days of filing season opening; refunds after manual filing typically take a few weeks if no verification is needed.
